Consider Supabase regional replicas (post-MVP). Add a 'privacy rating' visual (like privacy report cards) so users understand their data handling vs peers.

findings.description: Font selection: NeoDunggeunmo (Korean pixel font) is primary. While it supports Latin, Vietnamese diacritics (ă, ê, ô, ơ, ư) and Indonesian are not explicitly tested. Fallback to system font on unsupported chars. RTL support absent (not relevant for id/vi, but signals incomplete i18n maturity).
findings.suggestion: QA: render id/vi text in capture, consent, jarvis screens on iOS/Android. Verify diacritics render crisply. Consider Noto Sans CJK as fallback (supports all SE Asian scripts). Update DESIGN.md to document font fallback chain for id/vi.

findings.context: Indonesian/Vietnamese student/young professional (14-25, high data usage)
findings.description: App assumes reliable internet; no explicit offline-first UX. Capture → cloud-sync → no visual feedback on sync status. Users in areas with intermittent 4G (common outside major cities) may lose work or see confusing 'unsaved' states. No transparent local caching visualized.
findings.suggestion: Add sync-status badge (connected ✓ / pending ⏳ / error ✗) visible on capture and home screens. Show 'X entries queued' when offline. Implement robust queue (entries+attachments persist locally until ACK). In id/vi launch copy, set expectations: 'Works offline; syncs when connected.'

findings.description: No 'family role' or 'community context' framing. Persona screens ask about career/hobbies/identity, but not family duties, elder care, community standing (critical in SE Asian self-concept). Jarvis (SecondB) reframes as personal coach only; no mention of how insights serve family/work/society.
findings.suggestion: Add optional 'Context' question in onboarding: 'I'm navigating this mainly for: (myself / my family / my work / my community).' Tailor Jarvis tone accordingly. Show how each Pattern Core (Bond, Growth, Narrative, etc.) connects to user's stated context. Test with married-with-kids segment.

findings.description: AI 'Advisor' (Jarvis/SecondB) is brain-tier (premium) only. Free-tier users limited to 2 journal entries, 2 notes, 2 self-context prompts. Paywall copy in English; no regional context ('This costs $X in your country'). For Indonesia (avg income $200/mo), $10/mo premium feels prohibitive; users may abandon.
findings.suggestion: Set IDR/VND-specific pricing (e.g., IDR 25k ≈ $1.50/mo for soma tier). In id/vi consent, show: 'This feature is premium. Upgrade for unlimited journaling.' Test freemium models (cap completeness, not count: free = 5 min journaling, premium = unlimited). Monitor conversion in SEA; if <2%, pivot to ads or sponsorships.

findings.description: Sharing consent asks 'Allow XPRIZE judges to read this' and 'Allow public display on our site.' For young women in conservative contexts, any data persistence feels risky (family discovery, romantic partner control, social shaming). Consent UI is binary (yes/no); no gradation or review before publish.
findings.suggestion: Add 'draft sharing' flow: allow write-up, then 'review before any person outside you sees it' checkpoint. Emphasize in id/vi: 'You stay in control. We never share without your explicit OK.' Add option to auto-redact names/dates before sharing (privacy-safe variant). Test with female-focused focus groups.

findings.context: 중동(사우디/UAE) 전체 - OAuth provider 가용성 부족
findings.description: 현재 Google, Apple, Kakao, Naver만 지원. Kakao, Naver는 중동에서 작동하지 않음(한국 기반). Google, Apple은 작동하지만 사우디에서는 Gmail 계정이 정부/가족 모니터링 우려 때문에 보수적임. 중동 사용자들은 토지은행(رقم التليفون) 또는 대형 이메일(Outlook.sa, Yahoo Arabia)을 선호. 안드로이드에서는 Naver 버튼이 disabled되면 사용자 혼란.
findings.suggestion: 1) Kakao/Naver 버튼을 지역별로 숨기기(isProviderAvailableInRegion check) 2) 이메일/비밀번호 sign-up 강화 (OAuth 없어도 가능하게 이미 됨) 3) 향후: Microsoft OAuth 추가(Outlook.sa 연동) 또는 토지은행 기반 인증 파트너십.

findings.context: 사우디/UAE 여성 사용자 - 성별 민감성과 '관계' 표현
findings.description: 앱의 5가지 Pattern Core 중 하나인 'Bond Core(관계와 사랑)'는 서구 심리학 기반의 romantic/intimate relationships 중심. DESIGN.md의 voice tone '관계'는 개인적 친밀함을 암시. 사우디에서는 unrelated male-female 상호작용이 문화적으로 제한되고, 공개적으로 '사랑'을 표현하는 것은 금기. 'Bond Core' 마스콧(Relia)도 Western cute aesthetic이라 현지 이해 떨어짐.
findings.suggestion: 1) 아랍어 로케일에서 'Bond Core' 재정의: '관계'를 familial/professional relationships 중심으로 프레이밍 ('가족과의 유대', '직업상 신뢰', '공동체와의 연결') 2) 'Relia' 마스콧의 시각적 현지화: Islamic geometric patterns 또는 더 중립적 디자인으로 재해석 3) 질문 다시쓰기: '당신의 가장 신뢰할 수 있는 관계는?' (intimate가 아닌 trust 기반).

findings.description: 2nd-Brain은 개인의 내적 성찰('나를 안다'), 자기 개선(self-improvement)을 중심으로 설계됨. DESIGN.md 보이스: '몇 줄의 솔직함이 며칠치 흐름을 정리해줘요'. 하지만 중동(이슬람) 문화에서 정체성은 개인이 아닌 가족/종교/공동체 중심. '내가 누구인가'보다 '내 역할은 무엇인가'(son/daughter, worker, believer)가 중심. 자기 성찰이 self-indulgence(자기 중심성)로 해석될 수 있음.
findings.suggestion: 1) 아랍어 onboarding 재해석: '나'(self)보다 '우리'(family/community) 관점으로 재프레이밍. 예: '당신의 조각' → '당신이 맡은 역할의 조각' 2) 저널링 프롬프트 문화화: '오늘 나를 배웠다' 대신 '오늘 내 책임을 어떻게 다했나' 또는 '내 신앙이 오늘 나를 이끌었나' 3) 집단주의 프레임: persona 결과를 '나의 강점'이 아닌 '나의 역할과 책임' 관점으로 표현.

findings.description: 앱의 모든 자기성찰은 심리학(Big Five, MBTI, attachment theory) 기반. 이슬람 신앙, 종교 실천(salah/기도 시간), 쿠란 성찰, 영적 성장 추적이 전혀 없음. 중동 사용자에게는 정체성의 중요한 부분인 종교가 앱에서 완전히 보이지 않음. 라마단, 하지(hajj), 종교 공휴일 추적도 없음.
findings.suggestion: 1) 영적 저널링 모드: '오늘의 쿠란 성찰', '기도 일관성 추적', '영적 도전' 등 옵션 추가 2) 이슬람 달력(Hijri calendar) 통합 + 라마단/무하람/라일랏 알-카드르(Laylat al-Qadr) 마크 3) 종교 심리학 프레임워크: Islamic Psychology 기반의 질문 세트(예: Quranic character study, spiritual resilience) 4) 종교 커뮤니티: 공유 설정에서 '신앙 공동체와 공유' 옵션.

findings.context: 사우디/UAE 청소년(14-18) - 디지털 ID 및 가족 감시
findings.description: 중동 부모들은 자녀의 디지털 활동을 Absher(사우디 정부 앱), 휴대폰 통제 앱으로 감시. 10대 여성이 개인 일기 앱을 설치하면 부모가 적발할 수 있고, 앱이 보호자 동의 없이 진행되면 신뢰 문제. 또한 사우디에서는 18세 미만이 독립적으로 Supabase 계정을 가질 법적 권리가 불명확함.
findings.suggestion: 1) 보호자 모드: 부모/보호자가 자녀 계정을 감시하는 '투명한' 옵션(신뢰 기반) 2) 프라이버시 모드: 공식적 보호자 동의 없이 '프라이빗' 저널링 모드 제공(암호화, 부모 접근 불가) 3) 법적 명확화: 사우디/UAE 미성년자 디지털 권리 변호사 검토 후 법적 statement 게시.

findings.context: 중동(사우디/UAE) 전체 - 데이터 센터 및 GDPR 부재
findings.description: Supabase(캐나다) + Google Gemini(미국)을 사용. 중동 규제 요구사항(특히 사우디: SDAIA 규정, UAE: ADIB 데이터 보호법)에 따라 개인 데이터가 중동(Saudi/UAE) 데이터센터에만 저장되어야 할 수 있음. GDPR 같은 이슬람-네이티브 데이터 보호 규제가 명확하지 않음. consent에서 'overseas transfer' 동의를 강제하지만 대안이 없음(로컬 처리 옵션 없음).
findings.suggestion: 1) AWS Middle East (Bahrain) 리전 또는 로컬 Supabase 파트너 평가 2) 데이터 위치 선택: 가능하면 사우디/UAE DC에서만 처리하는 옵션 제공 3) SDAIA/ADIB 준수 statement 추가 + 로컬 법무팀 검토 4) 장기: Gemini 대체(on-device LLM) 고려.

findings.context: 중동(사우디/UAE) 전체 - 콘텐츠 검열 및 종교 민감성
findings.description: 사우디는 종교경찰(GCAM)과 콘텐츠 규제가 엄격함. 사용자가 종교 비판, LGBTQ+ 컨텐츠, 정치 비판을 일기에 쓸 수 있음. 앱이 이를 Google에 보낸다면(Gemini processing), 사우디 정부가 국제적 데이터 요청(data requests)을 통해 접근할 수 있음. 현재 앱은 종교/정치 민감 콘텐츠에 대한 경고나 로컬 암호화가 없음.
findings.suggestion: 1) 민감 콘텐츠 감지: GCAM 규제 콘텐츠(종교/정치)를 자동 감지하고 사용자에게 경고 2) 옵션: 민감 항목은 로컬에만 저장, Gemini 처리 스킵 3) 정부 요청 투명성: 연간 투명성 보고서에서 사우디/UAE 데이터 요청 공개 4) 법적 명확화: 사우디/UAE 사용자 약관에서 정부 접근 명시.

findings.context: Mexico City professional, 32, mid-level tech literacy, family-oriented
findings.description: Onboarding voice is individualistic ('your thoughts become a small map' / 'you understand yourself'). Copy doesn't acknowledge group/family context common in Mexico culture. No mention of sharing with trusted people or collective learning.
findings.suggestion: Research Mexican variants of self-understanding messaging. Add optional 'share with trusted person' workflow in capture/journal. Acknowledge that reflection often happens in conversation with family/mentors in Spanish copy.

findings.context: Brazilian educator, 35, concerned about data costs on mobile plan
findings.description: Image OCR and file upload features assume high-speed data. Large file uploads (PDFs, images) without bandwidth warnings. Brazil has expensive/metered mobile plans in many regions. No data-saver mode or compression settings.
findings.suggestion: Add data saver toggle in settings. Compress images before upload. Show estimated data cost before OCR/file capture. Cache locally first, sync when WiFi available. Document which features use least data.

findings.context: Mexican designer, 28, visual culture, shares openly on socials, expressive
findings.description: Journal mode has no visual expression (emoji, image-first, color coding). Text-heavy UI. No gallery view of past entries. OCR exists but no 'save sketch/drawing and caption it' workflow common in visual journals. Copy is clinical ('today's piece', 'reflections').
findings.suggestion: Add visual journal modes: photo-first entry, emoji reactions to feelings, color-coded mood timeline. Show gallery of past entries with date/sentiment color. Use warm, relational Spanish copy ('Mi momento hoy' vs 'Today's piece'). Highlight visual storytelling in marketing.

findings.context: Monterrey college student, 23, active on TikTok/Instagram, community-oriented
findings.description: Language toggle is on sign-in only, not visible in settings/account. To switch from English to Spanish, user must sign out and back in. No Spanish localization exists; fallback to English. Settings UI is sparse, lacks 'Accessibility' section (font size, dyslexia-friendly fonts).
findings.suggestion: Move language picker to settings. Pre-load Portuguese/Spanish fonts (Dyslexie or similar for accessibility). Add font-size control. Implement proper localization for all screens (not just auth). Auto-detect device language and ask 'use Portuguese?' on first-run.

findings.context: Mexican artist, 29, emotional expression, spirituality, values reflection
findings.description: Journal prompt is 'today's piece' (clinical). ESM (emotion state measurement) offers 'context' (alone/with people/moving) and 'energy' (scale) but no emotion words (joy, longing, grief, peace). Crisis routing uses US 988 (not available in Mexico). No spiritual/meaning-making framing even though common in Mexican culture.
findings.suggestion: Add Spanish emotion lexicon (alegría, nostalgia, paz interior, asombro) to ESM. Reframe journal as 'Mi reflexión de hoy' (warm). Add optional 'What does this teach me?' (meaning-making) vs. 'What does this problem need?' (coaching). Add Mexico crisis hotlines (CVT Teléfono de la Esperanza 5563-24-39). Test messaging with Mexican spiritual practitioners.

findings.context: São Paulo retiree, 45, trusts institutions, basic digital literacy, limited income
findings.description: No accessibility features (font size <14px on small screens, no high contrast). Email/password form requires 8+ mixed-case + number (good security, bad for older adults). No 'Email me a magic link' option (easier than password). No Portuguese translation means instructions are in English. FAQ/help is missing.
findings.suggestion: Add font-size settings (14–24px range). Offer email-link sign-in as alternative to password. Create Portuguese user guide with screenshots. Test with older Brazilian users (SESC programs). Add 'Forgot password' email template in Portuguese. Consider accessibility mode with larger touch targets and reduced motion by default.

findings.context: Bangalore, 35yo, startup founder, high-income, power-user, wants self-knowledge integration with work
findings.description: App captures journal + assessments but no native integrations (Notion, Obsidian, GitHub, Slack for context). User wants: (1) bi-directional Obsidian sync (pull notes, push persona), (2) weekly Slack digest ('You wrote most about: relationships'), (3) export-to-Claude for RAG. Currently, only manual export via persona screen (share as JSON/PDF). Power-user leaves after 2 weeks, defeated by manual workflow.
findings.suggestion: Phase 2: Add Zapier/Make.com integration templates (low-code bridges). Priority 1: Obsidian plugin (read daily-notes folder, sync tags, import/export). Priority 2: Weekly email digest (Resend) with top tags + mood trend. Priority 3: OpenAI API key field in settings, auto-export persona JSON as 'instructions.txt' for custom GPT context. Requires backend /integrations endpoints.

findings.context: Mumbai, 32yo female, teacher, spiritual/religious, joint family, marriage prospects under family negotiation
findings.description: App's self-discovery framing is individualistic ('know yourself', 'your center'). User's identity is embedded in family/community roles (daughter, future-wife, educator). Journaling about identity crisis or relationship doubts feels selfish in cultural context. Safety lexicon forbids 'therapy/counseling' but user conflates self-reflection with seeking therapy — guardian might interpret app use as sign of mental-health crisis. Persona assessment (MBTI, Big Five, attachment styles) feels like 'labeling yourself' — taboo in collectivist culture that values role-fulfillment over personality expression.
findings.suggestion: Reframe app copy in consent/onboarding: 'समझें अपनी शक्तियां और भूमिकाएं' (Understand your strengths and roles, not just personality). Add cultural-context prompts: 'How do you balance personal goals with family expectations?' Add family-role persona variant (daughter/wife/professional roles) alongside individual types. Emphasize use-case: 'Track how you grow in your roles' not 'Discover your true self'. In Hindi copy, use 'आत्मचिंतन' (self-reflection) not 'आत्मबोध' (self-realization, which implies spiritual awakening).

findings.context: Bangalore, 26yo female, tech worker, high English literacy, LGBTQ+ identity, exploring sexuality/gender
findings.description: User wants to journalize identity exploration but fears data breach exposing LGBTQ+ status in India (Section 377 context, though decriminalized, social stigma remains). App's safety system flags 'therapy/counseling' terms in yellow (rephrase hint) but doesn't explain *why* or offer reassurance. Privacy policy is English-only, dense legal text; user can't verify whether Supabase (backend) shares data with India-based authorities. No explicit LGBTQ+ inclusion language in app copy or mascot personas.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit LGBTQ+ inclusion statement in privacy.md: 'Your identity is yours. We do not disclose sexual orientation, gender identity, or relationship data to third parties. Data is encrypted in transit and at rest.' Add section to consent flow: 'Safe space for identity exploration — your entries never appear in shared content (personas, insights).' Implement 'Hide from all insights' toggle per entry (so sensitive entries don't get analyzed/tagged). Expand persona avatars to include queer/non-binary coded mascot variants (visual inclusion).

findings.context: Tier-2 city, 55yo male, retired, digitally new, WhatsApp-dependent, grandchildren in house, wants to share learnings with family
findings.description: User is digitally new; app assumes smartphone fluency (swipe to navigate, tap modals, etc.). UI uses gesture-heavy design (swipe between tabs, long-press to edit). Onboarding carousel uses swipe-only navigation (no 'Next' button). User taps screen multiple times looking for button. Primary motivation is sharing life wisdom with grandchildren, but app is private-first; no 'family album' mode. Export persona as JSON is useless to non-technical user (can't open or share on WhatsApp).
findings.suggestion: Add explicit 'Next >' buttons alongside swipe on onboarding. Reduce gesture dependency (add back-button nav). Implement 'Export as PDF' option (pretty format, can email/WhatsApp). Add 'Create family post' workflow: select insights, add photo, auto-generate shareable image (text + QR linking to public read-only persona). Implement haptic feedback on tap to confirm action registered (accessibility for motor uncertainty).

findings.context: Tier-2/3 city, 24yo, college student, ₹0 income, borrows parents' phone, data-conscious (200MB/day budget from family Jio plan)
findings.description: App loads high-res art assets (island sprites, mascot animations, UI illusts) on every screen. Persona page with AI-generated persona card is heavy (image + metadata). No data-saver mode. User on 2G/weak 4G watches screens load 10+ seconds. Capture screen hangs when photo upload (OCR mode) tries to encode to cloud. User burns 50MB/mo on this app alone; parents restrict usage. App becomes unusable mid-month when data quota depleted.
findings.suggestion: Implement 'Data Saver' toggle in settings (compress images to 40KB thumbnails, skip art on slow networks, defer sync). Lazy-load mascot sprites (only when in focus). Add image optimization on device (resize OCR photos to 800x600 before upload). Cache static assets (onboarding art, persona templates). Show data usage indicator per operation: 'Upload will use ~2MB, current: 45MB used this month.' Implement progressive-image technique (LQIP: low-quality image placeholder, then load full).

findings.context: Metro, 40yo, CMO/brand professional, high-income, Indian market expert, evaluating app for brand partnerships
findings.description: App markets globally with English/Korean copy. No India-specific positioning. User researches 'Is this app culturally aware of India?' — finds no Hindi, no UPI, no Indian payment method, no Indian regulatory compliance messaging (GDPR/CCPA copy but no India-specific data law alignment: DPDP Act, RBI guidelines, ISP rules). Privacy policy is generic; user cannot verify whether Indian regulators have been consulted. Lack of localization signals app is not serious about India market.
findings.suggestion: Create India-specific landing page (landing.html, route /in): highlight Hindi support, UPI payment, DPDP compliance, Indian team/advisors. Add footer: 'Compliant with India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023.' Publish blog: 'Why we built 2nd-Brain for India.' Partner with Indian wellness influencers (yoga teachers, motivational speakers) for credibility. Ensure privacy.md explicitly mentions: data centers (AWS Mumbai region), no cross-border transfer without consent, RBI compliance for any future financial features.

findings.context: Mumbai/Bangalore, 28yo female, therapist/counselor, using app for personal growth, evaluating for client recommendations
findings.description: App explicitly forbids 'therapy', 'counseling', 'diagnosis' in UI copy to avoid regulatory issues. However, therapist recognizes assessment tools (MBTI, ECR-S, Big Five) are psychology frameworks *of the kind used in therapy*. User is confused: are these clinical? The UI says 'not clinical' but uses validated psych tools. Concerns: (1) Can she recommend to vulnerable clients? (2) Will app comply if client in crisis? (3) Does app meet Indian mental-health regulation if used therapeutically? Lack of clarity makes therapist hesitant to advocate.
findings.suggestion: Add 'For Professionals' page in settings explaining: 'Assessment tools are based on peer-reviewed frameworks (ECR, BFI) but 2nd-Brain is not a diagnostic tool — it's a self-knowledge app. For clients in crisis, refer to licensed counselors. 2nd-Brain can complement therapy, not replace it.' Include therapist-specific copy in consent: 'If you're a mental-health professional recommending this app, your clients agree they are responsible for their own well-being; 2nd-Brain provides tools for insight, not treatment.' Add disclaimer: 'In India, this app does not constitute professional mental-health service under AIACP/psychology licensing.'

findings.description: Free tier가 journal 2회, note 2회는 적어 보일 수 있지만, 실제로 '일기는 주 1-2회 쓰는 사람'에게는 충분할 수도 있음. Gemini의 free quota도 충분하면 upgrade 동기가 부족. UI가 '제한'을 강조하지 않으면, 사용자는 '이걸 왜 돈을 내고 쓰지?'라고 생각할 수 있음.
findings.suggestion: Capture 화면에서 journal/note를 쓸 때마다 '당신은 이번 달 2회 중 X회를 사용했습니다' 메시지를 prominent하게 표시. 또는 progress bar (2/2 다 사용함). 또는 2회 다 사용한 후 'Premium으로 업그레이드하면 무제한으로…' 메시지. 현재는 제한에 도달해야만 알 수 있음.

findings.context: 명상/자기 성찰가 (35~60세) - 깊이, 긴 대화, 안내된 성찰
findings.description: Chat UI가 짧은 Q&A 중심 (quick actions 칩). Advisor의 '깊이 있는 질문' 능력 불명확. Persona 분석이 심리적 깊이를 제공하는가 불명확 (MBTI/Big Five는 있지만 자기 성찰 가이드 없음)
findings.suggestion: Jarvis에 'Modes' 추가: Analytic/Divergent 외에 'Reflective' mode → Advisor가 open-ended questions 제시 (e.g., '지난주 가장 의미 있던 순간은?'). Persona 화면에 'reflection guide' 섹션 추가: 결과를 바탕으로 명상/성찰 prompt 제시

findings.description: Free tier에 광고가 없음 (좋음). 하지만 명시적으로 '광고 없음' 정책 표시 없음 → brain tier와 free tier의 차이 불명확. 또한 offline-first 구조인데, 구독과 offline 기능의 관계 불명확 (sync가 soma+만 가능? brain만?)
findings.suggestion: Settings에 '광고 & 개인정보' 섹션 추가: 'All tiers ad-free' 명시. 또한 'Sync & offline' 설명: free=local only, soma+=cloud sync, brain=multi-device sync + backup. 이를 통해 각 tier의 가치 명확화

findings.context: 한국 30-50대 직장인 - 빠른 UX와 오프라인 작동에 대한 기대
findings.description: 한국 사용자는 앱 응답성(로딩 시간)에 매우 민감. 특히 직장에서 점심시간 10분 내에 일기 기록, 인사이트 확인을 기대. 또한 지하철/터널 같은 오프라인 환경에서 로컬 저장 후 나중에 동기화 패턴을 원하나, 현재 코드에서 실시간 저장 강제인지, 오프라인 작동 가능 범위가 불명확.
findings.suggestion: 앱 문서/매뉴얼에 '오프라인 작동 가능한 기능'을 명시: '일기/메모는 저장되지 않을 때까지 로컬 임시 저장' 같이. 분명한 저장 상태 인디케이터 추가: 회색 체크(로컬 저장), 민트색 체크(클라우드 동기). 온보딩 또는 매뉴얼 스크린에 '오프라인 팁' 추가: '지하철에서 기록하고 연결되면 자동 저장'. 성능 프로파일링해서 capture 화면 로딩 < 2초 목표 설정.

findings.context: Stuttgart engineer, 35, structured, data-focused (Germany) - mid-to-high income
findings.description: Account deletion flow requires typing 'DELETE' in English. For German users, cognitive friction increases in non-native language when executing critical action. No German-language alternative. Sign-up password rule (8+ chars) is stated but no guidance on German special characters (ü, ö, ä) support.
findings.suggestion: Accept localized confirmation phrases (German: 'LÖSCHEN'). Validate password field handles all UTF-8 (German umlauts). Add field help text in user's language explaining security rules. Test with German keyboard inputs.

findings.description: Capture screen offers 5 modes (journal, memo, link, OCR, file) plus wiki track toggle plus classification UI. No simplified 'power user' vs 'casual' mode. High-income professional users may find feature density overwhelming without clear mental model in their language.
findings.suggestion: Add onboarding quiz ('What's your primary use?') to gate mode visibility. Remember user preference ('Mostly journal + memos'). For French/German users, offer 'Essential' mode hiding OCR/file initially. Provide mode explainer in native language.

findings.context: Nice artist, 26 (France) - creative, interested in wellness
findings.description: App explicitly forbids 'therapy', 'healing', 'diagnosis' language to maintain product positioning (per DESIGN.md). French wellness culture sometimes conflates journaling with therapeutic benefit. If user writes content implying emotional distress, no localized crisis support (safety hotline) is visible for France. Crisis router shows US/Korean hotlines only.
findings.suggestion: Add French-language crisis resources (SOS Amitié: 09 72 39 40 50, Suicide Écoute: 01 45 39 40 00) to CrisisRouter. Ensure crisis modal translates to French. Document this in consent notice. Clarify in French copy that app is NOT therapy but may trigger reflection.

findings.description: UI heavily uses Korean design language ('cosmic pixel village', cute robot mascot SecondB with pixel art). German design aesthetic typically favors minimalism, sans-serif (Helvetica, DIN), clean geometry. Pixel art + cosmic metaphors read as playful/childish rather than professional to German audience.
findings.suggestion: Offer design theme variant for German/French markets: 'Professional' mode removes pixel art, replaces 'village' with 'network', uses clean sans-serif fonts (Inter, DM Sans). Keep Korean cosmic palette but simplify mascot (reduce cuteness, increase modernity). A/B test with German beta users.

findings.context: Berlin startup worker, 28 (Germany) - technical, growth-focused - mid-high income
findings.description: Profile data is not shown to user anywhere in exported/shareable form. German professionals expect to review/verify what data is being collected (transparency principle). No 'Download my data' (GDPR Article 20 - data portability) option visible in settings. Persona export is mentioned in consent but no clear 'Export Profile' button.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Download my data' button in account settings. Implement GDPR Article 20 export: JSON with all records, wiki pages, persona, usage stats, consent log. Ensure export is in English/German. Test with German DPA. Show file size/download time estimate.

findings.description: App assumes smartphone literacy (OCR, file uploads, markdown clips). No beginner-friendly 'text-only' mode. Capture modes (memo, OCR, file) present technical jargon ('Markdown', 'DOCX'). Limited screen real estate on budget phones may render capture UI confusing.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Simple Mode' toggle (reduces capture to: voice memo, typed text, photo). Simplify UI copy for id/vi ('paste text from app' vs 'Markdown clip'). Test on Redmi/Poco devices (budget phones dominant in SEA). Offer voice journaling as primary capture mode (reduces friction for non-typists).

findings.description: Age verification floor is 14; under-14 requires 'verifiable guardian consent' (marked as PIPA-compliant but not implemented). For adults signing up, DOB is still required (security best-practice). UI copy doesn't explain why; can feel intrusive in privacy-conscious cultures.
findings.suggestion: Explain briefly in auth screens (id/vi): 'We ask your age to keep teens safe. Your birth date is never shared or used for marketing.' For under-14 flow (when shipped), integrate with local digital ID systems if available, or accept guardian consent via email (simpler MVP, less liability).

findings.description: App's metaphors (Soul Core, spiritual mascots) are neutral, but no acknowledgment of diverse belief systems. Some users may feel alienated ('this is too spiritual for me') or over-fit to non-dominant religion. No alternative framing (e.g., 'Core Self', 'Personal Hub').
findings.suggestion: Offer theme/framing choice in settings: 'How do you like to think about yourself: Spiritual / Practical / Scientific?' Adjust mascot names + language tone accordingly (kept low-impact; cosmetic only). Low priority unless UXR reveals concern.

findings.context: 중동(사우디/UAE) 전체 - 임상 용어 제약(clinical vocabulary prohibition)
findings.description: 앱이 심리치료/진단 용어('mental health', '정신건강') 금지하는 정책이 있으나(lexicon.ts), 이는 영어/한국어 기반. 아랍어 추가 시 금지할 '임상 용어'가 다를 수 있음. 예: 'الصحة النفسية' (psychological health)는 중동에서 널리 받아들이지만, 'العلاج النفسي' (psychotherapy)는 stigma. 문화별로 조정 필요.
findings.suggestion: 1) 아랍어 safety lexicon 추가: cultural-sensitive clinical terms 정의 2) 중동 문맥에서 '안전한' 용어: 'تطوير الذات' (self-development), 'الوعي الذاتي' (self-awareness), 'النمو الروحي' (spiritual growth) 3) 각 로케일별 금지 용어 테스트 확대.

findings.context: 중동(사우디/UAE) 전체 - 이미지 및 캐릭터 현지화 부재
findings.description: 앱의 그래픽 스타일(Cosmic Pixel Graph Village, SecondB mascot, worker sprites)이 Western cute aesthetic(anime/pixel art style). 중동 사용자에게는 이질적. Islamic geometric patterns, Arabic calligraphy, 중동식 icon style(Saudi design language)가 없음. 'SecondB'라는 이름도 영어 기반이고 아랍어 컨텍스트에서 의미가 없음.
findings.suggestion: 1) 마스콧 이름 현지화: SecondB → 'ثاني عقل' (Thani Aql / 두 번째 지혜) 또는 이슬람식 명칭 2) 비주얼 현지화: 마스콧과 아일랜드를 Islamic geometric patterns, Arabic calligraphy로 재디자인(선택적 로케일) 3) 아이콘 스타일: Western pixel art에서 Middle Eastern design language로 일부 아이콘 재해석. Codex와 협력.
